items.title: Life Water's earnings forecasts revised higher on stronger margins
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Life Water Bhd appears increasingly well-positioned to deliver stronger earnings through financial years 2026 and 2027, MBSB Research said. Following a recent visit to its plant, MBSb Research said Life Water had confirmed a 60 sen per carton average selling price (ASP) increase effective May 2026, with a potential second 60 sen hike hike in July should PET resin prices remain elevated. Reflecting the confirmed May ASP increase and expectations for a second hike in July, MBSB Research revised upwards Life Water's earnings forecasts for FY26, FY27 and FY28 were raised by 5.7 per cent, 10.2 per cent and 11.9 per cent respectively.

items.title: Bukit Aman dismantles Langkawi drug syndicate, 191 suspects arrested in 231 raids since April 10
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — The Bukit Aman Narcotics Crime Investigation Department (NCID) dismantled a drug distribution syndicate in Pulau Langkawi with the arrest of 191 individuals, including several ‘key figures’ in Op Langka. He said 231 raids were carried out in Pulau Langkawi and the Klang Valley, involving various offences under the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952. “As such, aggressive action will be taken under the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, Dangerous Drugs (Special Preventive Measures) Act 1985, and the Security Offences (Special Measures) Act 2012 (Sosma),” he said.

items.title: Policeman collapses during marching drill at Sepang IPD, 36‑year‑old corporal pronounced dead at scene
items.summary: SEPANG, May 14 — A policeman died after collapsing during a marching drill at the Sepang district police headquarters (IPD) here yesterday. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar and Selangor Police Family Association (Perkep) chairperson Datin Rozana Kasbon conveyed their condolences to the family of Khairil Idzhar following his passing. “The passing of Cpl 171014 Khairil Idzhar is a great loss to the Selangor police contingent.

items.title: JPJ: Honda to recall 430 motorcycles nationwide after safety issues detected in handle locks and wiring harnesses
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 14 — Boon Siew Honda Sdn Bhd will recall 430 units of Honda motorcycles, models CMX500A and CBR650RAC, starting May 25 following the detection of technical issues that could potentially affect vehicle safety. Road Transport Department (RTD) director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli, in a statement yesterday, said the recall involves models identified as affected for inspection and corrective action on related components. “All vehicle owners involved will be contacted by Boon Siew Honda Sdn Bhd for the process of coordinating their vehicle inspection slots.

items.title: Southwest Monsoon begins today, Zahid urges Malaysians to brace for hotter days, sudden storms
items.summary: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said this period typically brings lower rainfall distribution as well as hotter and drier weather conditions across most areas. “I would like to advise the public to remain aware of current weather developments, use water prudently and avoid any open burning activities that could lead to haze and environmental pollution,” he said in a Facebook post yesterday. Ahmad Zahid, who is also National Disaster Management Committee chairman, said heavy rain, strong winds and thunderstorms could still occur in certain areas during this period.

items.title: Investing in stocks with moats for success
items.summary: Brand strength An economic moat can arise from several sources. Companies with powerful brands enjoy customer loyalty and trust that competitors struggle to replicate. Cost advantages Cost advantages also create moats.

items.title: WHO confirms eight cases of Andes hantavirus in outbreak
items.summary: GENEVA: Eight people infected in the hantavirus outbreak aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship have tested positive for the Andes virus, the only strain transmitted between humans, the World Health Organization said on Wednesday. "Eight cases were laboratory-confirmed for Andes virus (ANDV) infection, two are probable, and one case remains inconclusive and undergoing further testing," the UN health agency said in its latest update on the outbreak. All known cases in the current outbreak were people aboard the cruise ship.

items.title: Federal judge blocks US sanctions against UN expert on Palestinian territories
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A federal judge on Wednesday temporarily blocked US sanctions against Francesca Albanese, a UN expert on the Palestinian territories, finding that the Trump administration likely violated her free-speech rights by imposing the measures after she criticised US ally Israel's war in Gaza. The sanctions barred her from entering the US and banking there. Albanese has described sanctions as part of a broader US strategy to weaken international accountability mechanisms.

items.title: Foreigners take over vacant land for illegal farming, sparking outrage
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Foreigners have reportedly occupied vacant land near Jalan PJU 10 in Damansara Damai to run illegal farms, sparking frustration among residents over the lack of enforcement. A check by Harian Metro found that the illegal farmers intentionally left tall trees standing around the perimeter to camouflage the activity as ordinary scrubland. A resident in her 60s, known only as Shahirah, said the majority of the foreigners involved were United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) cardholders.

items.title: Suspect in teen sodomy case has past conviction for rape, robbery
items.summary: District police chief Assistant Commissioner Ramsay Embol said checks found the suspect had previously been convicted over the offences and jailed in 2008. "Checks confirmed the suspect has a previous criminal record as stated and had served a prison sentence," he said when contacted today. Closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage later captured the victim walking with the suspect at 8.05am.

items.title: US House passes bill allowing year-round sales of E15 gasoline
items.summary: 1346 bill, or the Nationwide Consumer and Fuel Retailer Choice Act, approved by a vote of 218 to 203, would permit fuel retailers to offer E15 year-round, removing seasonal restrictions linked to smog concerns. Supporters say allowing year-round E15 sales would expand biofuel demand and help lower fuel prices that have spiked since the start of the Iran war. The Congressional Budget Office estimates the bill would increase direct spending by US$2.7 billion while raising revenues by US$0.4 billion, resulting in a net deficit increase of about US$2.3 billion between 2026 and 2036, based on an assumption that the legislation would take effect in August 2026.

items.title: US children's book author sentenced to life after poisoning husband
items.summary: LOS ANGELES: An American woman who made headlines by writing a children's book about grief after poisoning her husband was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole on Wednesday, US media reported. Prosecutors said Richins killed her husband, Eric Richins, in 2022 by serving him a cocktail laced with five times the lethal dose of fentanyl — allowing her to inherit US$4 million and receive another US$2 million from life insurance policies she secretly took out on him. "I'm broken, broken without your dad, broken without you boys," she said in court on Wednesday, the Tribune reported.

items.title: Vance says US making progress' on Iran deal
items.summary: I think that we are making progress," Anadolu Ajansi reported Vance told reporters when asked if the US is still engaging with the Iranians after Trump called Tehran's response "unacceptable." "The fundamental question is, do we make enough progress that we satisfy the president's red line? Asked about Trump's remarks that he does not think about "Americans' financial situation" when negotiating with Iran, Vance responded: "I think that's a misrepresentation of what the president said."

items.title: Malaysia's MRO sector on course for RM55bil target [WATCH]
items.summary: MALAYSIA's aerospace maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) industry is accelerating towards its RM55 billion revenue target by 2030. He said Malaysia's MRO segment has emerged as the backbone of the national aerospace industry, generating high-value jobs for more than 12,000 workers while anchoring the country's ambitions to become a regional aerospace powerhouse. Sim said Malaysia could not afford to stand still as regional rivals expand their aerospace capabilities.

items.title: 'Miraculous': 11 plane crash survivors rescued at sea off Florida
items.summary: LOS ANGELES: All 11 people aboard a private airplane that crashed at sea off central Florida survived the wreck and were rescued from their lifeboat hours later by a US Air Force Reserve team, US Coast Guard and Air Force officials said on Wednesday. The survivors ultimately were hoisted to safety by the crew of a hovering rescue helicopter, dispatched from the Air Force Reserve's 920th Rescue Wing from a base near Melbourne, and were flown to Melbourne Orlando International Airport for medical attention, officials said. Air Force and Coast Guard officials said they had no immediate information about the extent of injuries and medical conditions of the survivors.

items.title: South Carolina lawyer Murdaugh's murder conviction overturned
items.summary: NEW MEXICO: South Carolina's highest court on Wednesday overturned the murder conviction of former lawyer Richard "Alex" Murdaugh, who was serving two consecutive life sentences for allegedly shooting his wife and 22-year-old son dead in June 2021. The decision followed a February request by Murdaugh's attorney for a new trial. South Carolina Attorney General Alan Wilson said his office would retry Murdaugh as soon as possible.

items.title: Warsh confirmed as Fed chair as central bank faces Trump assault
items.summary: The Senate voted 54 to 45 in favour of Warsh, with Republicans holding a slim majority and ensuring President Donald Trump's nominee to replace Jerome Powell was confirmed. Once known as a monetary "hawk" against inflation, Warsh has shifted in line with Trump's push for lower interest rates that has posed an unprecedented challenge to the Fed's independence. "Warsh's biggest challenge will likely be dealing with President Trump," said David Wessel, senior fellow at the Brookings Institution.

items.title: Trump and Xi set for high-stakes talks in Beijing
items.summary: BEIJING: US President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping are set to tackle thorny issues dividing the two superpowers when they meet in Beijing on Thursday, with Iran, trade and Taiwan on the cards. The US president arrived for the two-day summit on Air Force One late Wednesday accompanied by top CEOs, including Nvidia's Jensen Huang and Tesla's Elon Musk — symbols of business deals Trump hopes to reach. On Friday, Trump and Xi are set to have tea and a working lunch before the US president heads home to Washington.

items.title: US jury begins deliberations on 737 MAX victim suit against Boeing
items.summary: NEW YORK: A Chicago jury began deliberations on Wednesday afternoon in a suit brought against Boeing by family members of a 24-year-old American who perished in a 2019 Boeing 737 MAX crash. The suit was brought by relatives of Samya Stumo, who died in the March 2019 Ethiopian Airlines crash, the second of two MAX calamities that together claimed 346 lives. The trial featured testimony from Stumo's relatives, including father Michael Stumo, who said the disaster still haunts the family.

items.title: Income labels fail to reflect Malaysians' real financial struggles
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysians can now check where they stand under the country's latest household income classification by the Statistics Department amid renewed public debate over income groups and fuel subsidy targeting. Under the department's Monthly Gross Household Income Threshold by Household Group and State 2024 report, released last October, households are classified according to income deciles across every state. These new income thresholds have attracted greater attention amid ongoing discussions on the RON95 fuel subsidy and possible changes to eligibility criteria for higher-income households.

items.title: What is Pinduoduo and how is its bargain-driven model reshaping Malaysians’ shopping experience?
items.summary: Founded in 2015 by Shanghai-based PDD Holdings, Chinese e-commerce giant Pinduoduo has turned e-shopping into digital bargain-hunting, where prices fall further as more users participate. From group discounts to referral links, Pinduoduo’s social group-buying model blends sharing with aggressive discounting, making securing the lowest price into a “group sport”. A ‘group sport’?

items.title: Israel hammers Lebanon with strikes, killing 22
items.summary: BEIRUT: Lebanon's health ministry said 22 people including eight children were killed on Wednesday as Israel intensified strikes on the country, with several deadly raids hitting south of Beirut. The state-run National News Agency (NNA) said Israeli airstrikes had pounded around 40 locations in Lebanon's south and east. The health ministry said late Wednesday that 10 people including six children had been killed in strikes on three south Lebanon villages.

items.title: Nigerian ex-energy minister gets 75-year jail term for fraud
items.summary: LAGOS: A Nigerian court on Wednesday sentenced former power minister Saleh Mamman to 75 years in jail for siphoning off millions of dollars from hydro-electricity projects. Nigeria's anti-corruption agency, the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC), said Mamman was found guilty of laundering 33.8 billion naira (US$24.6 million). Several other top officials from Buhari's government are facing trial for fraud, including a former justice minister, an ex-central bank governor and a former labour minister.

items.title: US renews offer of US$100mil to Cuba if it cooperates
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States on Wednesday renewed an offer of US$100 million in aid for Cuba, pressuring its longtime nemesis to cooperate as it weathers an economic crisis that includes prolonged blackouts. The State Department on Wednesday publicly renewed the proposal, which comes after the United States piled new sanctions against key parts of Cuba's state-controlled economy. The United States, the statement said, was working to promote "meaningful reforms" in Cuba.

items.title: UAE 'denies reports' of secret visit by Israeli PM Netanyahu
items.summary: DUBAI: The United Arab Emirates said Thursday it "denies reports" that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu recently visited the country, after Netanyahu's office said he had held a secret meeting with the UAE president. "The United Arab Emirates denies reports circulating regarding an alleged visit by Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to the UAE, or receiving any Israeli military delegation in the country," the UAE foreign ministry said in a statement. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Azam: I wanted to ensure support for new MACC chief [WATCH]
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said he meant no ill will when he reminded graft-busters not to "sabotage" the new chief. Azam said he wanted to ensure stability and continuity in MACC during the leadership transition. "I wish the new chief commissioner well and hope the commission will fully support his leadership."

items.title: Pahang crown prince summits Kinabalu
items.summary: KUNDASANG: Tengku Mahkota of Pahang Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah reached the summit of Gunung Kinabalu at 8.45am yesterday. Tengku Hassanal and his entourage began their final ascent at 3.07am after arriving at Panalaban at 3.45pm on Tuesday. Gunung Kinabalu, standing at 4,095.2m above sea level, is the highest mountain in Malaysia and is located within the 754 sq km Kinabalu Park.

items.title: Thailand to halve visa-free stays to 30 days
items.summary: BANGKOK: Thailand will cut the visa-free stay period for tourists from 93 countries from 60 days to 30 days to help prevent criminal activity, the foreign minister said. Foreign Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow said on Tuesday the Foreign Affairs Ministry will submit a plan to the cabinet for approval to reduce the visa-free stay period for foreign tourists in Thailand to 30 days from 60 days. Thailand has set a visa-free period for tourists from 93 countries since July 2024.

items.title: UEM channels RM5.5b to Bumiputera vendors
items.summary: UEM Group Bhd and its subsidiaries have awarded over RM5.5 billion in procurement contracts to Bumiputera vendors and suppliers between 2023 and 2025 to strengthen Bumiputera participation in the economy. UEM said yesterday the spending had benefited more than 5,000 Bumiputera enterprises involved in infrastructure and engineering-related sectors. Its motorway unit, PLUS Malaysia Bhd, allocated 90 per cent of its procurement budget to Bumiputera businesses during the period, while its asset and facility management arm, UEM Edgenta Bhd, channelled 49 per cent of its procurement spending to Bumiputera vendors.

items.title: Air India cuts some international routes as fuel prices bite
items.summary: MUMBAI: Air India said Wednesday it would reduce or cut several international routes over jet fuel price hikes and airspace restrictions due to conflict. Air India did not give the total number of flights affected, but said it would suspend routes to Chicago, Shanghai, Male and Singapore from certain Indian cities including New Delhi, Mumbai and Chennai between June and August. The airline added that it would also cut the frequency of flights to San Francisco, Paris, Milan and Sydney.

items.title: Indonesia seeks 18-year sentence for ex-education minister and Gojek co-founder over US$125mil graft case
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesian prosecutors said on Wednesday that they were seeking an 18-year prison sentence for Nadiem Makarim, a former Indonesian education minister and co-founder of ride-hailing firm Gojek, over his involvement in a corruption case. Makarim created tender specifications that only fit the Chrome system to "make Google the sole controller of education ecosystem in Indonesia", prosecutors alleged. In a hearing on Wednesday in a Central Jakarta corruption court, prosecutors said Nadiem's role in the case while serving as a minister violated the government's anti-corruption pledges and harmed the education system.

items.title: China cybercrime ringleader extradited to South Korea in US$25mil BTS-linked hack case
items.summary: SEOUL: A Chinese alleged cybercrime ringleader, whose victims reportedly included BTS megastar Jungkook, was extradited from Thailand to South Korea on Wednesday over a hacking scheme that stole more than US$25 million, Seoul's justice ministry said. The suspect was arrested in Bangkok in an Interpol-led operation and flown to South Korea's Incheon airport Wednesday in cooperation with Seoul's National Police Agency, it said. Victims reportedly included celebrities, conglomerate chairmen and startup executives, with South Korean reports saying K-pop star Jungkook was among those targeted.

items.title: Thailand cuts visa-free stays from 60 to 30 days over crime concerns
items.summary: BANGKOK: Thailand will cut the visa-free stay period for tourists from 93 countries from 60 days to 30 days to help prevent criminal activity, the foreign minister said. Foreign Minister Sihasak Phuangketkaeow said on Tuesday that the Ministry of Foreign Affairs will submit a plan to the Cabinet for approval to reduce the visa-free stay period for foreign tourists in Thailand to 30 days from 60 days. Thailand has set a visa-free period for tourists from 93 countries since July 2024.

items.title: Beserah state rep calls for 'reset' to restore Cherating's tourism appeal
items.summary: KUANTAN: Once a globally renowned tourism destination, Cherating beach requires a rebranding exercise to redefine its niche and future direction. Datuk Andansura Rabu (Pas–Beserah) said the Pahang government needs to determine whether the destination should be developed as a modern beach tourism hub or one rooted in traditional identity. Andansura proposed a roundtable discussion involving all stakeholders to find amicable solutions and determine Cherating's future direction, rather than relying solely on one-off events.

items.title: Immigration detains 74 foreigners in large-scale enforcement operation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 74 foreigners were rounded up by the Immigration Department during a large-scale operation on Tuesday, exposing employers who allegedly used valid business licences as cover to hire undocumented workers. Immigration deputy director-general (operations) Datuk Lokman Effendi Ramli said the raids involved several business premises, including restaurants, retail shops, barber shops, laundrettes, kiosks and supermarkets. "A total of 59 immigration officers were deployed to several locations in Puchong, involving inspections at 30 premises, 186 foreigners and 175 locals," he said in a statement.

items.title: Ulu Bendul park closed after four suspected leptospirosis cases detected in Negeri Sembilan
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 13 — The Negeri Sembilan Health Department (JKNNS) has detected four suspected cases of leptospirosis, or rat urine disease, at the Taman Eko Rimba Ulu Bendul recreational park in Kuala Pilah. JKNNS said one patient was admitted to hospital while three others received outpatient treatment after showing symptoms including fever, headache and muscle pain. It said a risk assessment found the environment at the recreational park conducive to the spread of leptospirosis, a zoonotic disease caused by Leptospira bacteria that can spread through contaminated water, soil or food.

items.title: Sabah postpones federal border agency rollout over immigration autonomy fears under MA63
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 13 — The Sabah government has decided to postpone the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) policy and implementation until it receives assurances that the move will not erode the state’s special immigration powers and autonomy. Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said Sabah’s immigration autonomy, together with that of Sarawak, was non-negotiable as it formed part of the safeguards enshrined under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63). He said the state government would not have direct authority over the agency, except through the Sabah Immigration director as provided under Subsection 6(3) of the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency Act 2024 (Act 860).

items.title: Ex-Astro employee jailed four years for converting 731 customer accounts to corporate access
items.summary: Checks on the Enterprise Data Warehouse (EDW) and CRM systems revealed that 731 regular customer accounts had been unlawfully converted into corporate accounts using the accused’s two user IDs between October 30, 2013 and August 20, 2020. The accused’s actions were not authorised by Astro, as corporate accounts were non-paying accounts created solely for the company’s internal use, while regular customers were required to subscribe through paid customer accounts instead of accessing Astro services through corporate accounts without authorisation. Under the charges, Nora Idayu, who worked in the Commercial Support Unit, was accused of tampering with data by converting 731 regular customer accounts into corporate accounts, despite knowing that the act would result in unauthorised changes to the database contents.

items.title: Five Malaysians rescued after vessel sinks off Greece, two detained for investigation, says Foreign Ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — Five Malaysians were rescued by the Greek Coast Guard after a vessel carrying them was involved in a shipwreck off the coast of Kastellorizo, Greece, on Tuesday (May 12). In a statement on Wednesday, the Foreign Ministry said preliminary reports indicated that the vessel experienced technical and navigational system failures during its voyage, causing it to strike a coral reef before sinking near the coast. The Foreign Ministry said it would continue monitoring developments in the case and issue updates as necessary.

items.title: Bukit Aman: RM5.3m wildlife parts seized in Kota Kinabalu antique shop raid, man arrested
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — Police arrested a man and seized various protected wildlife parts worth more than RM5.3 million during a raid at an antique shop on Jalan Gaya in Kota Kinabalu, Sabah, today. Bukit Aman Internal Security and Public Order Department’s Wildlife Crime Bureau/Special Intelligence Investigation (WCB/PSK) commander Datuk Mohd Zaki Ashar said the 53-year-old suspect, who is also the owner of the shop, was arrested during the raid at 9.45am. Mohd Zaki said all the items were kept without permits from the authorities, with the total value of the seizure estimated at RM5.32 million.

items.title: Najib granted stay on US$1.3b SRC payment order pending appeal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — The High Court here today granted Datuk Seri Najib Tun Razak a stay of execution on the payment of US$1.3 billion awarded to SRC International Sdn Bhd, pending his appeal against the court’s March ruling. Judge Datuk Ahmad Fairuz Zainol Abidin, now a Court of Appeal judge, ruled that special circumstances justified the stay. During a proceeding via Zoom today, Justice Ahmad Fairuz ordered Najib to pay RM2 million in costs to SRC International and RM150,000 each to five third-party defendants named in the suit.

items.title: MMEA expands Pulau Pangkor boat capsize search to 343 nautical square miles as seven still missing
items.summary: LUMUT, May 13 — On day three of the operation, authorities expanded the search radius to 343 nautical square miles for seven Indonesian undocumented migrants still missing after a boat capsized off Pulau Pangkor. State Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) director, Maritime Capt Mohamad Shukri Khotob, said the expanded area focuses on potential zones identified from earlier recoveries. “Today’s search focuses north of Pulau Pangkor, based on evidence that victims drifted there.

items.title: RTM to end Astro broadcast of TV1, TV2 and Okey from July 1 after pricing deal cannot be reached
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) will cease broadcasting its television channels on Astro’s platform from July 1 after nearly three decades of collaboration. “As such, RTM has decided to terminate the broadcasting services of RTM channels, namely TV1-HD, TV2-HD and Okey, on Astro’s satellite platform and over-the-top (OTT) platform operated by your party. RTM said rising channel rental costs had increased its financial burden in leasing the channels, making it more appropriate to end the service.

items.title: NRD: New MyKad rollout will not disrupt govt aid distribution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — The National Registration Department (JPN) has assured that the rollout of the new MyKad Structure will not affect the distribution of government assistance to the public. In a statement today, JPN said the transition and replacement of identity cards to the new MyKad Structure, which will be implemented this month, will not disrupt the redemption of government aid using MyKad, including Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (SARA) and Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95). The new security features on the MyKad Structure will also strengthen identity verification processes, it added, noting that the new card will continue to support the existing ecosystem used for subsidies and assistance distribution.

items.title: RON95, RON97 and diesel prices in Peninsular Malaysia lowered for May 14–20 period, says Finance Ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — Retail prices for RON97, RON95 without subsidy and diesel in Peninsular Malaysia will be lower for the May 14 to 20 period, with RON97 set at RM4.70 per litre, RON95 (without subsidy) at RM3.87 per litre, and diesel at RM4.87 per litre, the Ministry of Finance said. Under the revised pricing, RON97 is down 20 sen from RM4.90 per litre, while RON95 without subsidy is down 15 sen from RM4.02 per litre, and diesel in Peninsular Malaysia is down 30 sen from RM5.17 per litre. The government maintained subsidised prices for targeted groups, with RON95 under Budi95 remaining at RM1.99 per litre, diesel in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan at RM2.15 per litre, and diesel under SKPS and SKDS at RM2.05 and RM2.15 per litre respectively.

items.title: IGP: Cybercrime, online scams and cross-border threats need united response
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — Modern security threats such as cybercrime, online scams and increasingly complex cross-border crimes require strategic cooperation among various parties to be addressed effectively. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said losses due to online scams in Malaysia reached RM2.7 billion last year, highlighting that current security challenges cannot be handled by enforcement agencies alone. “The theme ‘Synergised Policing: Securing the Future Together’ highlighted during the PDRM Special Dialogue V 2026 (PSD V 2026) programme is highly relevant to today’s global security realities.

items.title: MACC denies blocking reports, says anti-graft agency open to all complaints
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 13 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) does not prevent any individual from lodging reports and remains open to receiving information related to corruption from any party, regardless of the individual’s position or background. The MACC stressed that allegations claiming the commission had prevented any individual from making a report were untrue. “The MACC is always open to receiving reports and information related to corruption from any party, regardless of the position or background of the individual involved.

items.title: Jho Low still under probe despite Trump pardon bid claims, says IGP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — Police are continuing investigations into fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low, said Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail. He said the case remained under police attention and action despite reports claiming that Jho Low was seeking a pardon from United States President Donald Trump. Mohd Khalid said the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) was also maintaining good relations with the relevant countries in efforts to continue investigations into the case.

items.title: Sungai Petani cops probe claim 13-day-old baby suffered fractured thigh during jaundice treatment
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, May 13 — Police have confirmed receiving a report regarding allegations that a 13-day-old baby boy suffered a fractured thigh bone while receiving treatment for jaundice at a hospital in this district recently. Kuala Muda police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said a police report was lodged by the baby’s father last Monday and investigations had since been initiated. The incident later went viral on social media, including photographs showing the baby receiving treatment at the hospital.

items.title: After Teluk Intan FRU crash, police tighten SOPs for large convoy operations, says IGP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) has tightened its standard operating procedures (SOPs) for large-scale convoy assignments to prevent a recurrence of the fatal accident involving a Federal Reserve Unit (FRU) truck in Teluk Intan, Perak, last year. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said the improvements place particular emphasis on compliance with SOPs by all personnel while carrying out their duties. In addition, Mohd Khalid said the PDRM is also continuously upgrading FRU logistics and equipment through procuring new vehicles funded by government allocations.

items.title: New MACC chief vows tougher crackdown on corruption, abuse of power
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 13 — Newly appointed Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman has vowed to intensify efforts to combat corruption, abuse of power and malfeasance, while pledging stronger enforcement, greater transparency and improved public trust in the agency. Abd Halim said restoring public confidence in the MACC was an urgent priority, stressing that the commission must remain firm, transparent and accountable while being fully guided by the rule of law, according to national news agency Bernama. Among the reforms proposed were tighter internal coordination, enhanced operational efficiency, strengthened recruitment of investigation officers and continued public education efforts on corruption prevention.

items.title: Penang has seven of 17 licensed e-waste firms, strong base to drive high-tech green economy says Bertam rep
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 13 — Penang has significant potential to turn waste into new economic wealth by attracting high-technology foreign investments as a new driver of the state economy. Datuk Seri Reezal Merican Naina Merican (BN-Bertam) said the state government should therefore formulate a comprehensive green waste and high-technology e-waste master plan. Reezal Merican, who is also chairman of Malaysia External Trade Development Corporation, also stressed the importance of waste-to-energy (WTE) projects, which could help resolve solid waste issues while generating energy.

items.title: Malaysia ‘very disappointed’ as Norway blocks missile export permit for LCS deal, says Fahmi
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 13 — Malaysia is “very disappointed” with the Norwegian government after it allegedly refused to issue an export permit for missiles already contracted by Malaysia, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil said today. “The Malaysian government is very disappointed with the actions of the Norwegian government, which did not approve the export permit for the delivery of missiles,” he said after the weekly Cabinet meeting. “The Malaysian government cannot accept the excuses given by the Norwegian government.

items.title: Baling mechanic claims trial to illegal possession of firearms
items.summary: Mohd Shahifuldin Mohd Shahiran, 46, pleaded not guilty to all charges read before Sessions Court Judge Rohaida Ishak, who also sat as magistrate. According to the first and second charges, he was accused of possessing a Colt UTC/TIC .45 calibre pistol and a magazine, as well as a Brazil-made Forjas Taurus S.A PT 24/7 9-millimetre pistol and a magazine without a valid licence or permit. For the third charge, Mohd Shahifuldin was accused of possessing a black imitation pistol made in Taiwan without lawful reason at the same date, time and place.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan leadership dispute: Court sets July 28 hearing on Dewan Keadilan dan Undang jurisdiction challenge
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 13 — The High Court today ordered the defendants in an originating summons filed by Datuk Mubarak Thahak and five others to file affidavits within seven days in support of their challenge to the court’s jurisdiction. Justice Roz Mawar Rozain issued the order after hearing preliminary objections raised by the first defendant, Negeri Sembilan Dewan Keadilan dan Undang (DKU) secretary Raja Norazli Raja Nordin, and the second defendant, the DKU. Counsel for both defendants, Steven Thiru, objected to the originating summons on the ground that the court lacks jurisdiction under Article 16(3) of the Negeri Sembilan State Constitution 1959.

items.title: Madani Mediation Centre to launch May 18, offers free dispute resolution for claims under RM250,000
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 13 — The government’s new Madani Mediation Centre initiative will be officially launched on May 18 in Kuala Lumpur, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil announced today. Fahmi said the initiative falls under the Prime Minister’s Department and is overseen by Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said. “In general, Mediasi Madani is a pro bono initiative involving commercial mediation,” he said after the weekly Cabinet meeting.

items.title: Communications minister clarifies report on BUDI95 cuts, says deputy finance minister misquoted
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 13 — The Cabinet has not discussed any proposal to cut the BUDI95 fuel subsidy quota, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil said today. He added that earlier reports quoting Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong as saying the government is looking to reduce the monthly BUDI95 allocation were inaccurate. “The Ministry of Economy studied the levels of 300 litres, 200 litres, 150 litres and 100 litres to see how many Malaysians fall within those categories,” he said.

items.title: Fahmi: Cabinet yet to discuss Jho Low’s US pardon bid, maybe next week
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — The Cabinet today did not discuss reports that fugitive Malaysian Low Taek Jho has sought a pardon from the United States President Donald Trump, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil said. The wanted businessman better known as Jho Low was reported by the Wall Street Journal (WSJ) to have filed for a pardon from Trump ‌weeks ago, citing ⁠people familiar ⁠with the ⁠matter. If ⁠granted, the US ⁠would purportedly remove criminal charges against Low.

items.title: All 104 World Cup 2026 matches to be shown on RTM, RTMKlik and Unifi TV, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — Malaysians will be able to watch all 104 matches of the 2026 Fifa World Cup through RTM and Unifi TV platforms, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil confirmed today. Speaking after the weekly Cabinet meeting, Fahmi said all matches from the expanded 48-team tournament would be available through RTM channels and streaming platform RTMKlik, alongside Unifi TV. “In line with the announcement I made last week, I would like to announce that all 104 matches will be broadcast on RTM and Unifi TV,” he said today during his weekly briefing.

items.title: Shah Alam police confirm wanted man in Bukit Kemuning teen assault case has history of rape, robbery and holding another captive
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 13 — A man being sought by police for allegedly confining and sexually assaulting a 14-year-old boy at an apartment in Bukit Kemuning, Section 32 here, has a previous criminal record involving rape, robbery and wrongfully confining a woman about 18 years ago. Shah Alam district police chief ACP Ramsay Embol confirmed that the suspect had been convicted of the offences and jailed in 2008. He said victim’s hands and feet were tied before he was allegedly sexually assaulted by the suspect.
